holdings.infoBox: YINN is an equity ETF with a total of 14 individual holdings.

overview.description: The Direxion Daily FTSE China Bull 3X ETF (YINN) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE China 50 Net Tax USD index. The fund provides daily 3x leveraged exposure to a market-cap-weighted index of the 50 largest Chinese stocks traded in Hong Kong. YINN was launched on Dec 3, 2009 and is issued by Direxion.
infoTable: FTSE China 50 Net Tax USD Index

data.title: China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon move against top Chinese tech firms
data.text: China is "strongly dissatisfied" with a U.S. move to add several large ​Chinese companies to the Pentagon's list of firms it says ‌are aiding China's military, the commerce ministry said on Saturday.

data.title: China's Producer Inflation Gains Pace as Mideast War Fuels Higher Prices
data.text: China's factory-gate inflation accelerated in May as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East kept fueling higher energy and commodity costs.

data.text: The U.S. has added some of the world's largest solar ​and battery manufacturers to a list ‌of companies it believes are aiding China's military.

data.title: Japan, China lead foreign government retreat from U.S. Treasuries as Gulf War fallout stokes currency fears
data.text: China reduced its stash of Treasury to $652.3 billion, the lowest level since September 2008. Japan, the single largest foreign holder, shed approximately $47 billion to $1.191 trillion.

data.title: China Factory Activity Expansion Slows
data.text: Factory activity in China expanded at a slower pace in April but beat expectations, suggesting limited pressure from surging energy prices as the Middle East conflict rumbles on.

data.title: China factory activity tops expectations in April — but growth slows as new orders soften
data.text: The official purchasing managers' index reading of 50.3, was higher than the the 50.1 expected by Reuters-polled economists.
